question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: NodeMap] Can an unprivileged external user craft a Rosetta API request that causes the server to route all transactions exclusively through a subset of nodes defined in NodeMap, effectively starving 30%+ of network processing nodes of traffic and causing them to be considered offline by the SDK?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Config.NodeRefreshInterval] Can an unprivileged external user trigger repeated network address book refresh cycles by exploiting the NodeRefreshInterval field, causing the SDK client to repeatedly re-evaluate node availability and mark 30%+ of nodes as unhealthy without brute force?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Pool.MaxOpenConnections] Can an unprivileged external user exhaust the database connection pool defined by Pool.MaxOpenConnections by sending concurrent Rosetta API requests, causing the mirror node to fail to serve 30%+ of node-related queries and effectively shutting down those processing paths?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Pool.MaxIdleConnections] Can an unprivileged external user cause connection starvation by holding open connections up to Pool.MaxIdleConnections, preventing new node-related DB queries from executing and causing 30%+ of node processing to stall?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Http.IdleTimeout] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a misconfigured Http.IdleTimeout (e.g., zero or very large value) by holding keep-alive connections open indefinitely, exhausting server goroutines and causing 30%+ of node-forwarding request handlers to become unresponsive?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Http.ReadTimeout] Can an unprivileged external user send slow-read HTTP requests that exploit a zero or unset Http.ReadTimeout, tying up server worker threads and preventing 30%+ of node-destined transactions from being processed?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Http.WriteTimeout] Can an unprivileged external user trigger slow-write conditions by exploiting an unset Http.WriteTimeout, causing the server to block on response writes and starving 30%+ of network node processing slots?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Http.ReadHeaderTimeout] Can an unprivileged external user send partial HTTP headers to exploit a zero Http.ReadHeaderTimeout, holding connections open and consuming all available server threads, causing 30%+ of node-bound requests to be dropped?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Cache.MaxSize] Can an unprivileged external user flood the entity cache defined by Cache.MaxSize with unique entity lookups, causing cache thrashing that degrades node query performance to the point where 30%+ of network processing nodes receive no valid requests?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Config.Online] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Config.Online flag behavior to trigger a mode switch that causes the SDK client to be initialized without a mirror network, effectively disconnecting 30%+ of network nodes from the address book?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Db.StatementTimeout] Can an unprivileged external user craft queries that exploit a zero Db.StatementTimeout, causing long-running DB statements to hold locks and block node-related data retrieval, effectively shutting down 30%+ of node processing pipelines?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Response.MaxTransactionsInBlock] Can an unprivileged external user request blocks with a very large MaxTransactionsInBlock value, causing the server to allocate excessive memory per response and starving 30%+ of concurrent node-processing goroutines of resources?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: NodeMap] Can an unprivileged external user submit a Rosetta /construction/submit request that targets only a specific subset of nodes in NodeMap, causing the SDK to mark all other nodes as failed and removing 30%+ of nodes from the active routing table?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Config.ShutdownTimeout] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a server shutdown sequence by causing an HTTP listen error (e.g., port conflict), and then exploit a very short ShutdownTimeout to prevent in-flight node requests from completing, causing 30%+ of nodes to receive incomplete transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Pool.MaxLifetime] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a very short Pool.MaxLifetime by triggering rapid connection recycling, causing DB connection churn that prevents 30%+ of node-related queries from completing within their statement timeout?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Db.GetDsn()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the sslmode=disable setting in GetDsn() to perform a man-in-the-middle attack on the DB connection, injecting malformed node data that causes the SDK to remove 30%+ of nodes from its routing table?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Config.Network] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Config.Network string (e.g., by triggering a config reload with a crafted network name) to cause the SDK client to connect to a different network where 30%+ of the expected nodes do not exist?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: NodeMap] Can an unprivileged external user send a malformed node endpoint string (e.g., IP:port with invalid format) via the HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_NODES environment variable path to cause nodeMapDecodeHookFunc to populate NodeMap with invalid entries, routing 30%+ of transactions to unreachable nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Config.NodeRefreshInterval] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a NodeRefreshInterval set to zero or a very small duration to cause the SDK to continuously refresh the network address book, creating a thundering herd of address book requests that overwhelms 30%+ of network nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Cache.MaxSize] Can an unprivileged external user set Cache.MaxSize to zero (if not validated) to disable entity caching entirely, causing every request to hit the DB and exhaust Pool.MaxOpenConnections, starving 30%+ of node-processing threads?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Pool.MaxOpenConnections] Can an unprivileged external user send a burst of concurrent /block or /transaction requests that each hold a DB connection for the full Db.StatementTimeout duration, exhausting Pool.MaxOpenConnections and causing 30%+ of node-related processing to queue indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Config.NodeVersion] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the NodeVersion string field (if reflected in network negotiation) to cause version mismatch errors that cause the SDK to reject 30%+ of network nodes as incompatible?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Feature.SubNetworkIdentifier] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the SubNetworkIdentifier feature flag to craft requests with mismatched shard/realm identifiers, causing the server to reject routing to 30%+ of nodes that belong to the correct sub-network?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Db.Host] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of SSL (sslmode=disable in GetDsn()) to redirect DB connections to an attacker-controlled host, injecting false node availability data that causes 30%+ of nodes to be marked as offline?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: NodeMap] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that NodeMap overrides the default network node list (when non-empty) to inject a sparse NodeMap with only a few entries, causing the SDK to route all traffic through those entries and effectively removing 30%+ of nodes from active use?
